questions.question: Explain the different types of tokens in C programming language with an example for each type.
keyPoints: Tokens are the smallest units of a C program
Types: identifiers, keywords, constants, operators, special symbols, strings
Identifiers: user-defined names for variables, functions, arrays (e.g., int marks;)
Keywords: reserved words with predefined meaning (e.g., int, if, while, return)

questions.question: Write a C program to demonstrate the use of type modifiers (short, long, signed, unsigned) with int data type. Show how each modifier affects the size and range of the integer type.
keyPoints: short int: typically 2 bytes, range -32768 to 32767
long int: typically 4 bytes, wider range
signed int: uses MSB for sign, includes negative values (default)
unsigned int: uses all bits for magnitude, 0 to 65535 (or higher)

questions.question: Explain the four storage classes in C (auto, register, static, extern) with respect to their storage, scope, lifetime, and default initialisation. Include code examples to illustrate each.
keyPoints: auto: default for local variables, stored in stack, automatic lifetime, not initialized
register: stored in CPU register (if available), faster access, cannot use & operator
static: retains value between function calls, scope within block/file, initialized to zero
extern: declares global variable defined elsewhere, file scope, entire program lifetime
Basic: int, char, float, double; Derived: arrays, pointers, functions; User-defined: struct, union, enum, typedef
Declaration: tells compiler about type and name (no memory); Definition: allocates memory
Output: First call: x=10, y=5, z=3; Second call: x=11, y=6, z=3 (z re-initialized, y retains)
Global x: data segment, static y: data segment, local z: stack (automatic allocation/deallocation)
